prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Alright, dear, imagine the CPU as the brain of the computer. It's like the boss that tells everything else what to do. It solves problems and makes decisions really, really fast. Without it, the computer wouldn't be able to do anything. It's hidden inside the computer, so you can't see it working, but it's always busy keeping everything running smoothly. Just think of it as the special part that makes sure your games and movies work!
